news.title: Taylor Foods May Not Be Responsible for Cyclospora Outbreak. Why Sweetgreen and Others Are Dropping.
news.summary: U.S. regulators walked back its findings about contaminated lettuce, saying that the Taylor Farms sample initially reported as positive for cyclospora was actually a false positive.

news.title: Sweetgreen Stock Soars as Diners Breathe a Sigh of Relief Over Cyclospora Outbreak
news.summary: Other food and restaurant stocks jumped, too, as investors likely hope that customers will resume their normal food-shopping habits.

news.title: Chipotle (CMG) Stock Looks Fully Priced Following Its 36% Slump
news.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill stock has had a tough year, with a steep share price decline set against valuation checks that suggest the current market price sits at a premium to its Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value, while earnings-based multiples look closer to fair. news.title: Factbox-US restaurant chains hit by foodborne illness outbreaks
news.summary: July 17 () - Foodborne illness outbreaks have repeatedly disrupted U. restaurant chains, triggering recalls, lawsuits and heightened regulatory scrutiny while pressuring customer traffic and sales.

news.title: Chipotle vs. Wendy's: Which Restaurant Stock Has the Edge Now?
news.summary: CMG's improving traffic, rewards growth and expansion plans give it an edge over WEN - but margin pressure and valuation still shape the debate.

news.title: Sweetgreen bounces back as Taco Bell takes the heat for produce panic
news.summary: Investors are finally digesting the lettuce panic, and salad chains are breathing a sigh of relief. Sweetgreen Inc. (NYSE: SG) caught a welcome 2.7% tailwind on Friday, beginning to claw back a brutal 23% slide over the past five days. The culprit for the recent freefall? Guilt by association. news.summary:
